rulings.Case Title: RESTAURANTS INTERNATIONAL, INC. A CORPORATION et al VS. NANCY MAR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 30, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANTS NANCY MAR, 1660 SACRAMENTO STREET ASSOCIATES, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion denied for first, second, and third causes of action, failure to shift burden of proof. The applicable statutes of limitations for the first and second causes of action were not raised as affirmative defenses and are waived. Motion denied as to third cause of action because motion denied as to second cause of action. Motion denied as to the fourth cause of action. Claim against Goodwin is barred by Anderson vs. Anderson (1995) 41 Cal.App. 4th 135, and Probate Code section 9353. However, Civil Code section 3439.08(b)(1) appears to allow suit against transferee of the fraudulent conveyance. Unclear whether Laidley vs. Heigho (1963) 326 F.2d 592 addresses the statutory scheme that is currently in place. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY OF MODESTO VS. AUTHORITY FOR CALIFORNIA CITIES EXCESS LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint, Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 30, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF CITY OF MODESTO Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Motion granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted to correct errors designated #2 in notice of motion. Motion denied as to #1, to add allegations of malicious, fraudulent and oppressive conduct claim for punitive damages. Delay not justified in view of facts known at time of filing of the complaint, lack of diligence in discovery, plaintiff's responses to discovery requests regarding punitive damages, and plaintiff dilatorily hiring expert. Prejudice to defendant who must prepare for January trial and complete discovery. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Order To Compel Uninsured Motorist Arbitration And To Appoint A Panel Of Arbitrators
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 30, 2009, line 11, PETITIONER NGOC TRINH Notice Of Petition For Order To Compel Uninsured Motorist Arbitration and To Appoint a Panel Of Arbitrators. Petition granted, no opposition filed. Court nominates three potential arbitrators: Judge Alex Saldamando, Judge David Lee, and Judge Margaret Kemp from the ADR Services, 50 Fremont Street, Suite 2110, San Francisco. Parties shall each strike one name within ten days and remaining panelist will hear the matter. The arbitration will be conducted within 60 days of the appointment of the arbitrator. =(302/CWW)
